name: Implement Task Classes

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Implement Task Classes is complete and correct

scope:
Implement behavioral prompt files for every class in the task classes spec. Reference: docs/specs/2026-03-15T00-04Z-task-classes.md.

Three phases, must execute in order:

PHASE 1: Refactor execute.md and create default.md.
Extract ALL behavioral guidance (Phase C coding conventions, Phase D validation rules) from internal/project/templates/prompts/stages/execute.md into a new class prompt at internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/default.md. After extraction, execute.md must be purely structural: phase ordering, boundaries, terminal markers, commit protocol, AAR writing. Zero language-specific or SWE-specific guidance should remain.
Wire default.md into ContextBuilder: when a task has no Class field (empty or unset), inject default.md as Class Guidance. When a class fails to resolve, fall back to default.md. No task should run without class guidance.

PHASE 2: Author all language, framework, and non-language class prompts.
For every class in the spec tables (20 languages, 22 frameworks, 9 non-language classes), create a .md file under internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/. Language files at top level (go.md, python.md). Framework files in subdirectories (typescript/react.md, python/django.md).
CRITICAL: ZERO wolfcastle-specific content in class prompts. No terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs, AARs, yielding, or system mechanics. Each prompt must be RESEARCHED (official style guides, current linters/formatters/test frameworks, modern paradigms). Language prompts REPLACE (not augment) default.md validation rules. Use 'prefer X' not 'always X'. 40-80 lines for languages, 30-60 for frameworks. writing.md references writing/voice.md separately. Framework prompts do not repeat language fundamentals.

PHASE 3: Wire defaults into config and validate.
Add task_classes entry to config.Defaults() for EVERY class with a prompt file (50+ entries). Wire ClassRepository.Validate() into daemon startup validation. Add --class validation to task add CLI (reject unknown values). Auto-assign Class 'audit' to audit tasks at claim time when their class is empty.

Phase 2 MUST complete before Phase 3 starts. Create children in order: Phase 1 leaf, Phase 2 orchestrator, Phase 3 leaf.
success_criteria: execute.md contains zero SWE behavioral guidance; all behavioral content lives in class prompts
Every task runs with a ## Class Guidance section (default.md fallback for unclassed tasks)
51+ class prompt files exist under internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/ with zero wolfcastle-specific content
config.Defaults() ships task_classes entries for every authored class prompt
Daemon startup validates class prompt availability and logs warnings for missing files
CLI task add rejects unknown --class values with helpful error
Audit tasks auto-receive Class 'audit' at claim time when unclassed

breadcrumbs.text: Created 3 children: refactor-execute-and-create-default (leaf, Phase 1), author-class-prompts (orchestrator, Phase 2), wire-defaults-and-validate (leaf, Phase 3). Ordering: Phase 1 extracts behavioral content from execute.md into default.md and wires the fallback, establishing the pattern all class prompts follow. Phase 2 authors all 51 prompt files (orchestrator will decompose into language/framework/non-language groups). Phase 3 wires config defaults and validation, which requires all prompts to exist first. Strict sequential dependency: 1 -> 2 -> 3.
